site.title: Kinglet Programming Language
site.description: Kinglet is a modern, statically typed programming language that compiles ahead of time to native code.
section.language.h2: Four system concerns, expressed in the static structure.
section.language.lead: Kinglet makes four questions visible in the program: what the code can do, who owns a resource, how an operation can fail, and whether every state has been handled.
feature.capability.title: Capability
feature.capability.body: Concepts describe the operations code requires. Concrete types are checked at compile time and specialized through monomorphization.
feature.ownership.title: Ownership
feature.ownership.body: Scalars, shared values, and resources follow distinct transfer rules. Resources use moves and explicit borrows to express lifetime responsibility.
feature.fallibility.title: Fallibility
feature.fallibility.body: Fallible conversions produce T?; ?: supplies a fallback, postfix ? propagates failure, and try / catch defines recovery.
feature.exhaustiveness.title: Exhaustiveness
feature.exhaustiveness.body: Postfix match destructures enums, structs, and arrays. The compiler checks state coverage for enums, booleans, and optionals.
section.code.lead: Nullable types, propagation with ?, and try / catch make error paths visible instead of implicit.
section.toolchain.h2: One compiler, from source to a native executable.
section.toolchain.lead: The bootstrap compiler covers parsing, semantic checking, Kinglet IR, LLVM lowering, a small runtime, and the command-line driver — all in one binary.
